description: Starter regression gold set for npm run engineer:eval. Replace runId/compareRunId with founder real runs before weekly iteration.

cases.question: Car has understeer mid corner — what should I try first on this run?
I lose the rear on entry. what are some small changes to try?
If I raise front upper inner shims 0.5mm, what happens to the handling?
What's the difference between raising under lower arm shims vs upper inner on the rear?
Summarize the main setup changes between my compare run and primary run and what handling effect they might explain.
Why might my best lap be slower than the previous session on the same tire run?
Which suspension parameters am I far from community median at this track grip level?
Given my tire run number, should I expect pace drop — what setup compensations are reasonable?
Hypothetically on a high-grip carpet track, would a stiffer rear spring usually help rotation or hurt mid-corner?
If grip drops mid-day, is adding droop always the right move?
I changed rear spring, rear camber, and rear toe between runs — how should I interpret handling now?
I want more bite on entry without killing mid-corner — which end do I tune first?
Should I go heavier or lighter on front damper oil if the car feels lazy on turn-in?
Explain anti-dive split on the front bulkhead and when I'd change FF vs FR pickup.
Based on my notes and handling ratings, what's the highest-confidence next setup experiment?
